description: Fix CLI UX issues discovered during fresh-user experience testing: misleading restart message when config port is unchanged, and generic port-in-use error that doesn't detect existing OpenTabs servers.

userStories.title: config set port: skip restart message when value is unchanged
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs config set port <N>` to not tell me to restart when the port hasn't actually changed, so that I'm not confused by unnecessary instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: When `config set port` is called with the same value already in config, it prints 'Port is already set to <N>.' (or similar) and does NOT print any restart message
When `config set port` is called with a value that differs from config but matches the running server's port, it prints 'Port is already set to <N>.' and does NOT print a restart message
When `config set port` is called with a value that differs from both config and the running server's port, it still prints the existing restart hint with the running port
When the server is not running and the value differs from config, it still prints the existing offline hint
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, lines 455-488 (handleSetPort function).

The current code compares `newPort` against `oldPort` (the config file's stored value). It has two branches:
1. `newPort !== oldPort`: Shows 'Server running on port X. Restart to apply port change.' — but if the running server's port happens to equal `newPort`, no restart is needed.
2. `newPort === oldPort`: ALWAYS shows 'Restart the MCP server for the port change to take effect.' — even though nothing changed.

Fix approach:
- After writing the config, compare `newPort` against the running server's port via `resolvePort(options)` (which already exists on line 478).
- If `newPort` equals the running port (or the config didn't actually change), print a no-op message like 'Port is already set to N.' and skip the restart hint.
- Only show the restart message when the new port genuinely differs from the running server's port.
- The `resolvePort` helper reads from config and falls back to the --port flag and DEFAULT_PORT, so after writing the config it will return the new value. Instead, capture the running port BEFORE writing the config, or hit the health endpoint to get the actual running port.

WARNING: `resolvePort(options)` resolves from the config file, not from the running server. After the config write on line 472, calling `resolvePort` will return the NEW port (just written), not the server's actual port. To get the running port, resolve it BEFORE writing the config, or query the health endpoint. The simplest fix: capture `const runningPort = resolvePort(options)` before the `atomicWriteConfig` call on line 472.

Test file: platform/cli/src/commands/config.test.ts — add a test for the no-change scenario.

userStories.title: start: detect existing OpenTabs server on port before showing generic error
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs start` to tell me when my port is already occupied by another OpenTabs server (not just 'port in use'), so that I know to stop it rather than switching ports.
acceptanceCriteria: When the target port has an OpenTabs server running (responds to GET /health with a valid JSON containing 'status'), the error message says 'OpenTabs is already running on port <N>' and suggests 'opentabs stop' (with --port if non-default)

When the target port is in use by a non-OpenTabs service (health check fails or returns unexpected response), the error message says 'Port <N> is already in use by another service' and suggests using a different port
The health check has a short timeout (1-2 seconds) so it doesn't delay startup when the port is occupied by a non-HTTP service
This works for both default port 9515 and custom ports
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts, lines 291-312.

The current code uses `isPortInUse(port)` (a TCP socket check) and then hardcodes two messages based on whether `port === 9515`. This is fragile — a user who sets a custom port in config doesn't get the OpenTabs-specific hint.

Fix approach:
- After `isPortInUse` returns true, attempt a GET request to `http://127.0.0.1:${port}/health` with a 1.5-second timeout (use the Node.js fetch API or the existing http module).
- If the health endpoint responds with valid JSON containing `status: 'ok'`, it's OpenTabs. Print: 'OpenTabs is already running on port <N>.' followed by 'Stop it with: opentabs stop' (add '--port <N>' if port !== 9515).
- If the health check fails or returns unexpected data, it's another service. Print: 'Port <N> is already in use by another service.' followed by 'Use a different port: opentabs start --port <number>'.
- Wrap the fetch in a try/catch so any network error (timeout, ECONNREFUSED, etc.) falls through to the 'another service' branch.
- Remove the current `port === 9515` ternary since the health check now handles all cases.

Existing pattern: The doctor command (platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts) already hits the /health endpoint to distinguish OpenTabs from other services. Look at how `checkMcpServer` works there for the fetch pattern. Reuse the same approach.

Do NOT import from doctor.ts — the health check logic should be inline or in a small shared utility. Keep it simple.
